meaning: Used to introduce an opinion based on your personal experience, job role, or viewpoint.
example: From my perspective, the new software is much easier to use than the previous version.

meaning: A formal phrase used to introduce your opinion.
example: In my view, the company should invest more in employee training.

meaning: Used to state your opinion or explanation of a situation.
example: As I see it, we have two main choices: expand the team or extend the deadline.

meaning: Used to show that your statement is based on information you have received, which might not be complete.
example: From what I understand, they are offering us a discount if we sign a two-year contract.

meaning: As stated by a person, document, or group (used to distance yourself from the statement).
example: According to the manual, the machine needs to be cleaned every week.

meaning: Used to show that you are giving your own opinion, rather than speaking for a group.
example: Personally, I prefer working from home, but I understand why some prefer the office.

meaning: Used to introduce your own opinion, especially when it might differ from others.
example: As far as I'm concerned, the project is finished once we submit this final report.

meaning: Used to state a common belief without naming specific people, making it sound objective.
example: It is widely believed that learning a second language improves cognitive skills.

meaning: Used to present an opinion as a common fact, reducing personal accountability for the claim.
example: It is generally accepted that regular exercise leads to better physical health.

meaning: Used to say that something is partly, but not completely, true.
example: I agree with your feedback to some extent, but I think the introduction is fine.

meaning: Partly; up to a point.
example: To a certain degree, success in business depends on luck as well as hard work.

meaning: Very probably; almost certainly.
example: In all likelihood, the manager will approve your vacation request since you asked early.

meaning: Very likely; used to state a strong expectation without complete certainty.
example: In all probability, the flight will be delayed due to the heavy snow.

meaning: Mostly; in general.
example: For the most part, the clients were happy with the demonstration we gave them.

meaning: In most situations; as a general rule.
example: Generally speaking, junior developers need more guidance during their first few months.

meaning: Without focusing on small details; generally.
example: Broadly speaking, our sales have increased, but a few products are not doing well.

meaning: Usually; as a habit or general practice.
example: As a rule, we do not reply to support emails over the weekend.

meaning: Used to say that you agree with the general idea of a plan, but there might be practical problems.
example: I agree with your proposal in principle, but we need to figure out where the funding will come from.

meaning: After considering all the facts or opinions.
example: On balance, I think the benefits of the merger outweigh the potential risks.

meaning: Used to make a general statement after thinking about all the aspects of a situation.
example: All things considered, the event was a great success, despite the technical glitches.

meaning: Usually or in most cases.
example: In general, we try to avoid scheduling meetings on Friday afternoons.

word: SO FAR AS I CAN TELL
meaning: Based on the evidence or information that is currently available.
example: So far as I can tell, the server is running normally and there are no active errors.

meaning: On the whole; generally speaking.
example: By and large, the new remote work policy has been received positively by the staff.

meaning: Used to say that you do not have enough information to be sure of something.
example: She might have left the company already, for all I know.

meaning: Used to state something that is obvious, though still stated to show alignment.
example: It goes without saying that we will need to work hard to meet this tight deadline.

meaning: Used to introduce a fact or point that makes your judgment of a situation more balanced or less critical.
example: The developer made a mistake, but to be fair, the requirements were extremely confusing.

meaning: Used to emphasize a statement or confirm something that has already been suggested.
example: The design is indeed very clean, but it might not appeal to older users.

meaning: Used when admitting that something is true, especially when it goes against your main argument.
example: Admittedly, the new process takes longer, but it reduces errors significantly.

meaning: A polite and slightly humorous phrase used to share your view, showing you do not think your opinion is better than others.
example: In my humble opinion, the original version was much more engaging.

meaning: In a way that can be imagined or believed; possibly.
example: We could conceivably launch the beta version by the end of next month if everything goes smoothly.
